formations_blocks: Belgium commanding possession and attacking from the start
Iran defending in a compact, counter-attacking posture
Iran trying to counter when they can
key_matchups: Trossard attacking down the left against Iran's defensive line
De Cuyper delivering from the left
phases.summary: Belgium begin the phase on the front foot, sustaining long spells of pressure and quickly turning broken Iranian attacks into renewed possession. Their attacks come through left-sided combinations and direct deliveries into the box, with De Bruyne and Lukaku both testing Beiranvand and Iran struggling to get out. Iran’s main response is a compact defensive effort and the occasional counter, but Belgium’s tempo and conviction make it feel like an early onslaught.

formations_blocks: Belgium boxed Iran in with quick combination play
Belgium building from wide areas and the left side of the box
adjustments: Meunier inverting to create an extra man on the last line
phases.summary: Belgium spent most of the phase pinning Iran back and trying to break a low block through quick combinations and wide delivery, with the main danger coming from Tielemans and Meunier’s right-sided involvement. Iran survived the pressure, then briefly stunned Belgium with Taremi’s disallowed goal before Belgium resumed attacking, now with Meunier inverting to add an extra runner on the last line.

This phase does not provide any tactical detail about formations, pressing structures, or in-game adjustments. The only football-related point is the half-time scoreline, Belgium 0-0 Iran, alongside a brief comment about Romelu Lukaku’s yellow-card risk and substitution suitability rather than any concrete tactical shift.

formations_blocks: Belgium attacking from a wide/right-sided set-piece phase after the break
Iran potentially shifting from a back four to a back five/defensive four after the Hardani–Jahanbakhsh change
adjustments: Iran substituted Hardani for Jahanbakhsh at 46', likely a shape adjustment with an extra attacker coming on for a defender
Trossard repeatedly moved inside before linking with De Bruyne on Belgium’s right-sided attacks
De Bruyne remained the main set-piece/crossing source for Belgium
key_matchups: Trossard vs Kanani (shot blocked after Trossard moved infield and fed De Bruyne)
Trossard vs an Iran defender on the foul that left him with a torn sock
De Bruyne’s deliveries vs Iran’s aerial defence/clearance line
Saelemaekers vs Iran’s back-post coverage on De Bruyne’s cross
phases.summary: The second-half opening period was dominated by Belgium, who kept working attacks through De Bruyne’s delivery from the right and Trossard’s drifting inside. Iran’s early substitution suggested a tactical reshuffle, possibly toward a back four, but Belgium still created the best chances from wide free kicks and corners, with Kanani and the back line repelling the danger for the moment.

formations_blocks: Iran chasing the game with more attacking numbers
adjustments: Iran made two attacking substitutions: Moghanloo on for Ghoddos, then Hosseinzadeh on for Ezatolahi
Iran repeatedly moved a striker on for a defender, increasing attacking risk
Belgium allowed Iran more space by dropping off and looking to protect the lead
key_matchups: Jahanbakhsh vs Belgium’s defending in the box
De Bruyne vs Iran’s defensive line on free-kicks and wide delivery
Castagne vs Iran’s defensive cover on the right-side cross
Tielemans vs Iran’s box defending at the near side of the cross
phases.summary: Iran, chasing a result, threw on extra attackers and pushed numbers forward, which in turn left them more exposed at the back. Belgium responded by sitting deeper and trying to exploit the space, while still creating moments through De Bruyne set pieces and Castagne’s excellent low cross; the biggest direct chance for Iran came when Ezatolahi’s long-range effort forced Courtois into an awkward save. The phase ended with Belgium still dangerous in transition/crossing situations and Iran gambling heavily for a goal.
